title: Fix machine ssh login failed in rootless mode
body:
add option to not read system ssh configurations
Fixes: #11731

Signed-off-by: pendulm <lonependulm@gmail.com>

[NO TESTS NEEDED]

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *pendulm* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **saschagrunert** after the PR has been reviewed. You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @saschagrunert` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Let's wait a bit until there's consensus on how to address the issue.  As mentioned in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11731#issuecomment-926460869 I am not sure whether `podman machine` should join the user NS.

podman machine command should not join userns, I think this is logically true.

`ssh -F none` is a quick fix, assume machine ssh should not use configuration from host. But this may have compatibility issue if -F option is not available in all version of ssh client.

I had test this on my Fedora and Mac, it seems OK.
Should you be running `podman-remote machine ssh` ?

> `ssh`

no this binary installed by default
